articles.title: Google Gemini CLI abused as a hacking agent, malware botnet operator
articles.summary: A Russian-speaking threat actor known as "bandcampro" used Google's open-source Gemini CLI AI tool as a hacking agent and to operate a small-scale botnet. [...]

articles.title: 23andMe reaches $18 million settlement with states for massive breach
articles.summary: A coalition of 42 state attorneys general reached an $18 million settlement with 23andMe for cybersecurity failings that led to a data breach.

articles.title: AsyncAPI npm packages infected with credential-stealing malware
articles.summary: Five malicious versions of AsyncAPI packages were published to the Node Package Manager (npm) in a supply-chain attack that delivered a remote access trojan with info-stealing capabilities. [...]

articles.title: OkoBot Malware Framework Injects Seed Phrase Phishing Into Ledger and Trezor Apps
articles.summary: A malware framework called OkoBot has been running on Windows machines since April 2025, and one of its modules is built to con hardware wallet owners out of their recovery phrase. On an infected PC, the request comes from inside the wallet's own desktop software. Sometimes it waits until you plug t

articles.title: Unpatched Cursor Vulnerability Exposes Users to Code Execution
articles.summary: An attacker can create a malicious repository containing a git.exe in the project root, and Cursor executes it automatically. The post Unpatched Cursor Vulnerability Exposes Users to Code Execution appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: CISA Urges Immediate Patching of Exploited SharePoint Vulnerabilities
articles.summary: Three vulnerabilities are actively exploited in attacks, including two that have been targeted as zero-days. The post CISA Urges Immediate Patching of Exploited SharePoint Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: We built a vulnerability vending machine: AI tokens in, zero-days out
articles.summary: Intruder built an AI-powered "vulnerability vending machine" that combines code slicing with LLMs to automatically discover complex software vulnerabilities. The company explains how the system found and exploited a previously unknown WordPress plugin zero-day, with additional discoveries already un

articles.title: Firefox, Chrome, Adobe, and VMware Updates Fix Multiple Critical Security Flaws
articles.summary: Mozilla has released updates to address two critical flaws in Firefox for which it warned that exploit code has been published. articles.title: Windows Bind Link Attacks Can Hide Malware From EDR Tools
articles.summary: Bitdefender researchers show how Windows bind links can create conflicting filesystem views to hide malware from endpoint security products. The post Windows Bind Link Attacks Can Hide Malware From EDR Tools appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Microsoft smashes Patch Tuesday record for second successive month
articles.summary: Vulnerability counts have been surging this year, and Microsoft's mammoth disclosure this week of 622 bugs is larger than the three previous months combined.

articles.title: Researcher Drops New Windows Zero-Day PoC Hours After Microsoft Patch Tuesday
articles.summary: Security researcher Chaotic Eclipse (aka Nightmare-Eclipse) has released a new proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit called LegacyHive. It has been described as a Windows User Profile Service arbitrary hive load elevation of privileges vulnerability. The Windows User Profile Service, also referred to as Pr

articles.title: Vulnerabilities Patched by Fortinet, Ivanti, ServiceNow
articles.summary: A critical security defect in the ServiceNow AI platform could allow remote attackers to execute arbitrary code. The post Vulnerabilities Patched by Fortinet, Ivanti, ServiceNow appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Cursor Flaw Lets Malicious Cloned Repositories Trigger Windows Code Execution
articles.summary: Open a repository in Cursor on Windows and, if a file named git.exe is sitting in the project root, Cursor runs it. articles.title: White House Launches AI-Driven ‘Gold Eagle’ Vulnerability Coordination Initiative
articles.summary: The new program stems from an AI-focused Executive Order signed by President Trump on June 2. The post White House Launches AI-Driven ‘Gold Eagle’ Vulnerability Coordination Initiative appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Progress Confirms Zero-Day Vulnerability Behind ShareFile Disruption
articles.summary: The company has rolled out a fix and is restoring access for Storage Zones Controller customers who apply it. The post Progress Confirms Zero-Day Vulnerability Behind ShareFile Disruption appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: CISA warns admins to patch actively exploited SharePoint flaws
articles.summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) warned Tuesday that attackers are actively exploiting three vulnerabilities to hack Internet-exposed on-premises SharePoint Server instances. [...]

articles.title: Compromised AsyncAPI npm Packages Deliver Multi-Stage Botnet Malware
articles.summary: Four compromised npm packages in the @asyncapi namespace have been observed distributing a multi-stage botnet loader, according to findings from OX Security, SafeDep, Socket, and StepSecurity. articles.title: US charges alleged operators of Russian bulletproof hosting service
articles.summary: U.S. federal prosecutors have unsealed charges against three Russian nationals, accusing them of providing bulletproof hosting (BPH) services to ransomware gangs that caused over $62 million in damages to victims worldwide. [...]

articles.title: Critical Vulnerabilities Patched With Fresh Chrome 150, Firefox 152 Updates
articles.summary: Public exploit code targeting the Firefox flaws exists, but no in-the-wild exploitation has been observed. The post Critical Vulnerabilities Patched With Fresh Chrome 150, Firefox 152 Updates appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.summary: SonicWall has warned of active exploitation of two zero-day vulnerabilities impacting Secure Mobile Access (SMA) 1000 series appliances, one of which could be exploited to achieve arbitrary command execution. articles.title: SonicWall Issues Urgent SMA Patch Warning for Two Zero-Day Exploits

articles.summary: SonicWall warns that threat actors have been exploiting two SMA1000 vulnerabilities, tracked as CVE-2026-15409 and CVE-2026-15410, in zero-day attacks and urges customers to install the newly released security updates. [...]

articles.title: Microsoft Patches Record 622 Flaws, Including Two Zero-Days Under Active Attack
articles.summary: Microsoft shipped its largest Patch Tuesday on record today, and two of the fixes close holes that attackers are already exploiting. articles.title: Spanish Police take down €140 million cyber fraud ring, arrest four
articles.summary: The Spanish Police dismantled a cybercrime and money-laundering organization that made €140 million ($160 million) from investment fraud and business email compromise (BEC) attacks. [...]

articles.title: Microsoft Patches a Record 570 Security Flaws

articles.title: Synopsys Finds No Evidence of Data Breach Amid Bosch Hack Claims

articles.title: Finland issues wanted notice for hacker behind massive psychotherapy data breach
articles.summary: The defendant's lawyer told Finnish media that he does not know where his client is but believes Kivimäki is outside Finland.

articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a previously undocumented Rust-based remote access trojan (RAT) codenamed LabubaRAT that masquerades as NVIDIA software to blend into target environments. articles.title: Progress confirms ShareFile zero-day flaw behind Storage Zone shutdown
articles.summary: Progress Software has confirmed that a high-severity zero-day vulnerability is behind the emergency shutdown of ShareFile Storage Zone Controllers last week and has released security updates to patch the flaw. [...]

articles.title: LastPass, Bitwarden users targeted with fake security alerts
articles.summary: LastPass is warning users about an ongoing phishing campaign that is using fake security notices to direct them to fraudulent websites. [...]

articles.title: NATO logistics, Ukrainian troops are top subjects of Russian camera hacks, advisory says
articles.summary: Dutch intelligence officials report that at least one Russian agency is compromising internet-connected cameras across Europe to spy on military logistics and Ukrainian personnel.

articles.summary: Two new phishing kits, Jalisco and OmegaLord, have been discovered in attacks targeting Microsoft 365 accounts, using techniques that defeat multi-factor authentication (MFA). [...]

articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered 11 old, Microsoft-signed, Unified Extensible Firmware Interface (UEFI) applications that could be abused to bypass Secure Boot on most systems using the modern firmware standard.
